description: The original document is a Judaeo-Arabic letter addressed to Eliyyahu the Judge, in Fustat, from his "family" (ahl baytih, which can also mean "wife"), in Alexandria. Little of the content is preserved, but it mentions something belonging to a woman named Rivqa. At the top of recto, in a different ink and different hand (the hand of Eliyyahu?), a few words in Judaeo-Arabic including the name Abū l-Ḥasan b. Abū l-[...].
